strategies.name: RSI(21) 50% Bounce Trend Following (Pullback to Role Reversal Zone)
strategies.description: A trend continuation strategy using RSI set to 21 periods combined with support/resistance role reversal and optional Fibonacci confluence. In an established uptrend, wait for price to pull back to a former resistance level now acting as support. Confirm with RSI(21) bouncing off the 50% zone (staying above 50 in an uptrend). Optional additional confluence from Fibonacci 61.8% retracement aligning with the support zone. Enter on a bullish confirmation candle at the support zone. Minimum 2:1 risk/reward. Add to position when price breaks above the prior swing high.

entry_signals.condition: Series of higher highs and higher lows confirming a bullish trend structure. Price trading above recent support zones.
entry_signals.value: higher highs and higher lows pattern

parameters.trend_structure: minimum 2 consecutive higher highs and higher lows

entry_signals.indicator: Role Reversal Zone (Former Resistance as Support)
entry_signals.condition: Price pulls back to a level that previously acted as resistance but now acts as support. This role reversal confirms the level's significance.
entry_signals.value: former resistance level being tested as support

parameters.zone_type: horizontal support/resistance from prior swing highs/lows
parameters.role_reversal: price approaches former resistance from above

entry_signals.condition: RSI(21) pulls back toward the 50% zone and bounces off it (staying above 50 in an uptrend). This confirms the trend is still intact.

parameters.bounce_condition: RSI approaches 50 from above and turns back up
parameters.invalidation: RSI breaks below 50 = trend may be reversing

entry_signals.condition: Fibonacci 61.8% retracement of the last swing aligns with the support/role reversal zone. This adds confidence but is not required.

parameters.fibonacci_type: retracement from most recent swing low to swing high
parameters.alignment_tolerance: Fib level within the S/R zone

entry_signals.condition: A bullish candle closes at or near the support zone, confirming buyers are defending the level. Examples: hammer, bullish engulfing.

exit_rules.condition: Minimum 2:1 risk/reward ratio reached. Example: $50 risk target -> $100 profit target.

parameters.calculation: distance from entry to stop x 2 = minimum profit target

exit_rules.condition: Price breaks above the prior swing high after initial entry. Add a second position for trend continuation.

parameters.action: add position on breakout of prior high

exit_rules.condition: Price breaks below the confirmation candle and support zone.
exit_rules.value: just below the confirmation candle / support zone

parameters.placement: tight stop below the zone to limit risk

additional_rules: If RSI breaks below 50 during pullback, trend may be reversing — consider switching to divergence reversal strategy
Stack multiple confluences for highest-probability entries: role reversal + RSI bounce + Fibonacci

filters.trend_required: established uptrend with clear higher highs/higher lows
filters.rsi_above_50: RSI must stay above 50 during pullback (trend intact)
filters.role_reversal: former resistance must be tested and held as support

missing_elements: Minimum number of higher highs/higher lows to define 'established uptrend'
Bearish version details (shorting pullbacks to resistance in downtrend)
How close RSI must get to 50 to count as a 'bounce'
assumptions: Confirmation candle = bullish candle closing above support zone
Role reversal = former resistance tested and held as support
Fibonacci drawn from most recent significant swing low to swing high
15-minute candle close used for entry timing
source_quotes: The 50% zone is the most critical level
Stack multiple confluences — divergence + support/resistance + Fibonacci + RSI 50 bounce
Use order blocks as targets — they represent levels where institutional buying/selling originated

strategy_names: RSI(21) 50% Bounce Trend Following (Pullback to Role Reversal Zone)
summary.extraction_notes: Well-defined conceptual framework with clear entry conditions and minimum R:R requirement. No backtest data provided. The multi-confluence approach (trend + role reversal + RSI bounce + Fibonacci) is thorough but may produce infrequent signals. Strategy 2 of 2 from this video.
summary.video_title: The Only 2 RSI Trading Strategies that REALLY Work
summary.primary_trading_style: Trend continuation with pullback entry at role reversal zones
summary.automation_feasibility: MEDIUM — Trend detection (HH/HL pattern) is well-defined. Role reversal detection (former resistance as support) requires historical swing point tracking. RSI(21) calculation and 50% bounce detection are straightforward. Fibonacci retracement is a standard calculation. The main challenge is programmatically identifying confirmation candles. Adding positions on prior high breakout is a simple condition.
summary.key_insight: The RSI(21) 50% bounce acts as a trend health check — if RSI stays above 50 during a pullback, the trend is intact and the pullback is a buying opportunity. If RSI breaks below 50, the trend may be reversing and you should switch to the divergence reversal strategy instead.
